original_hypothesis.title: Genesis Admin Can Drain Unclaimed Agent Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized` function in `Genesis.sol` allows the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` to withdraw any ERC20 token from the contract. This includes the `agentTokenAddress`. Since users must manually claim their agent tokens via `claimAgentToken`, the admin can withdraw these tokens before users claim them, effectively stealing user allocations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the admin is trusted, the contract code should enforce that user claims are fully backed. Allowing the admin to withdraw tokens that are actively owed to users violates the solvency invariant of the contract.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized allows DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to withdraw any token, including agentTokenAddress

preconditions: Genesis has succeeded and agent tokens are distributed
Users have not yet claimed their tokens
Admin acts maliciously or key is compromised

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: No check ensuring the withdrawn amount leaves enough balance to satisfy claimableAgentTokens

analysis_questions: Is there any off-chain mechanism preventing the admin from withdrawing user funds?
Can this be used to rug pull a successful genesis?
validation_requirements: Call withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized as admin for the agent token
Verify users can no longer claim their tokens

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After a highly successful genesis, the admin (or a hacker who compromised the admin key) calls withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized to drain all agent tokens from the Genesis contract. When legitimate participants try to call claimAgentToken, the transactions revert due to insufficient balance.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of user agent token allocations

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests verifying admin cannot withdraw claimed tokens

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
